REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT HUI WU, WEI TAN, SIMON TAN NOTICE OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; REQUEST FOR JUDICAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; AND DECLARATION OF CURTIS E. SMOLAR IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.10B FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE ANY VIOLATIONS WITHIN 1 YEAR OF THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.11A TO ALLEGE (1) WHETHER P IS ALLEGING A VIOLATION SOLELY BASED ON RETALIATION AND IS NOT SEEKING TREBLE DAMAGES, COMPARE PARAGRAPHS 181 AND 188; (2) IF PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING TREBLE DAMAGES, ALLEGING APPLICABLE VIOLATIONS WITHIN 1 YEAR OF THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT; AND (3) IF PLAINTIFF IS SOLELY ALLEGING VIOLATIONS BASED ON RETALIATION, ALLEGE RETALIATORY ACTS WITHIN 3 YEARS OF THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. UNLIKE IN MANEFEE, THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ARISE OUT OF THE VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.10B OR 37.11A, WHILE THEY MAY BE SUPPORTED BY SOME OF THE SAME FACTS THAT SUPPORT THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION. SEE MENEFEE V. OSTAWARI (1991) 228 CAL.APP.3D 239. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLE F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For A Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judical Notice In Support Of Motion For A Judgment On The Pleadings; And Declaration Of Curtis E. Smolar In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Cody Jaffe, Law office of Cody Jaffe, 415-524-3268, for plaintiff
Curtis Smolar, Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP, 415-986-5900, for defendant

Mr. Smolar contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HUI WU, WEI TAN, SIMON TAN NOTICE OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; REQUEST FOR JUDICAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; AND DECLARATION OF CURTIS E. SMOLAR IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the 2nd Cause of Action for violation of SFRO 37.10B for Plaintiff to allege any violations within 1 year of the filing of the complaint.
Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.11A to allege (1) whether P is alleging a violation solely based on retaliation and is NOT seeking treble damages, compare paragraphs 181 and 188; (2) if plaintiff is seeking treble damages, alleging applicable violations within 1 year of the filing of the complaint; and (3) if Plaintiff is solely alleging violations based on retaliation, allege retaliatory acts within 3 years of the filing of the complaint. Motion is otherwise DENIED. Unlike in Manefee, the remaining causes of action do not arise out of the violation of SFRO 37.10B or 37.11A, while they may be supported by some of the same facts that support those causes of action. See Menefee v. Ostawari (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 239

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charle F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
